raw.title: Embed Plate — Beam Bearing at CMU Face — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · AISC 360-22 §F11
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save 📂 Load 🖨 Summary 📄 Full Calc Embed Plate — Beam Bearing at CMU Face — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · AISC 360-22 §F11 Embed Plate — Beam Bearing at CMU Wall Face (ASD) (4) Horizontal Anchor Rods, 2×2 · Tension / Shear / Interaction · Bearing · Plate Bending · TMS 402-22 & AISC 360-22 TMS 402-22 §6.3 & §8.1.4.3 — Anchorage §6.3.2 Projected Apt §6.3.6 Min Embedment §8.1.4.3.1 Tension · §8.1.4.3.2 Shear · §8.1.4.3.3 Interaction §8.1.6 / §4.4.4 Bearing (0.33f′m) AISC 360-22 §F11 Plate Bending IBC 1605.2 — ⅓ Allowable Increase Print / Export PDF Save Inputs (.json) Load Inputs (.json) Anderson Rohr Engineering — TMS 402-22 / AISC 360-22 ASD ELEVATION/SECTION — PLATE / BEAM / WALL (CASE 1 GRAVITY) TYP. COURSING W/ HEAD JTS CMU WALL — SECTION PL t = 0.50" (2) per row @ Sx = 5.0" (2) per row @ Sx = 5.0" Lb = 6.00" BEAM W-SHAPE V P T (couple) C bearing Hp = 12.00" d = 9.00" et = 1.50" eb = 1.50" b = 7.625" (8" CMU) e = 2.00" (standoff) PLATE / ANCHOR DATA Wp x Hp x t = 8.00 x 12.00 x 0.50" Anchors: 2x2, Sx x d = 5.00 x 9.00" Lb (embed) = 6.00" e (standoff) = 2.00" Wall: 8" CMU b = 7.625" et/eb = 1.50/1.50" Hatch = grouted cell (typ. at anchor rows) ELEVATION/SECTION — CASE 1 GRAVITY (top row tension) WALL-FACE VIEW — FULL 2×2 ANCHOR GROUP APT WALL-FACE PLANE (projected) Lb Sx d diagonal overlap deducted in calc, not shaded ■ 1. Material Properties f′m — Masonry Strength (ksi) 1.5 ksi 2.0 ksi 2.5 ksi 3.0 ksi 3.5 ksi fy — Anchor Yield Stress (ksi) 36 ksi — F1554 Gr.36 / A36 55 ksi — F1554 Gr.55 60 ksi — Grade 60 Rebar 105 ksi — F1554 Gr.105 Fu — Anchor Tensile Strength (ksi) Typical: 58 (Gr.36) · 75 (Gr.55) · 125 (Gr.105). Capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi) internally. Fu,eff — Capped Value (ksi, auto) = min(Fu,input, 1.9fy, 125 ksi) Fy,plate — Plate Steel Yield (ksi) 36 ksi — A36 50 ksi — A572 Gr.50 ■ 2. Anchor & Plate Geometry TMS 402-22 §6.3 · AISC 360-22 §F11 ϕ — Anchor Diameter (in) 1/2″ rod 5/8″ rod 3/4″ rod 7/8″ rod 1″ rod 1-1/4″ rod nt auto (UNC coarse per ASME B1.1): 1/2″→13 · 5/8″→11 · 3/4″→10 · 7/8″→9 · 1″→8 · 1-1/4″→7 tpi — used in Eq 6-7 net area. Lb — Effective Embedment (in) Face of masonry to bearing surface of anchor head/nut (§6.3.1). Min = max(4ϕ, 2″) §6.3.6. Sx — Row Spacing (within a row) (in) Horizontal center-to-center spacing of the 2 anchors in a row. Drives Apt/Apv overlap deduction (§6.3.2). Wp — Plate Width (in) Horizontal plate dimension (bearing width & plate-bending width). Hp — Plate Height (in) Vertical plate dimension. et — Top Edge Distance (in) Top plate edge to top anchor row. eb — Bottom Edge Distance (in) Bottom plate edge to bottom anchor row. d — Row Spacing (computed) (in, auto) = Hp − et − eb (vertical distance between the two anchor rows). e — Eccentricity / Standoff (in) Wall face / anchor plane to beam bearing line. Drives the anchor-couple moment M = V·e (anchor-couple eccentricity only). ac — Bearing Block Depth (in) Assumed compression bearing-block depth at the plate edge (§4.4.4). Default 1″. b — Wall Thickness (sanity check) 6″ CMU — b = 5.625″ 8″ CMU — b = 7.625″ 10″ CMU — b = 9.625″ 12″ CMU — b = 11.625″ Embedment sanity only: warns if Lb ≥ b − 1.5″. lbe,shear — Free-Edge Dist. (shear) (in) Distance to the nearest free edge or opening in the direction of shear (below for gravity). Default 12″ — increase if the wall is continuous; enter the actual distance if an edge/opening is nearby. t — Plate Thickness (in) Drives plate bending & shear (AISC 360-22 §F11). ■ 3. Load Case 1 — Gravity / Down Top row tension · bottom edge bearing Enter ASD service-level loads. For gravity/down, the beam reaction V acts downward at standoff e, putting the TOP anchor row in couple tension and bearing at the BOTTOM plate edge. V1 — Beam Reaction Shear (kips) Total vertical reaction delivered to the plate (all 4 anchors). P1 — Axial Pull-out (kips) Uniform tension/pull-out shared by all 4 anchors (P/4 each), added to couple tension. Apply ⅓ allowable stress increase (IBC 1605.2) to Case 1 — k = 3/4 on the demand side of every check. Use only when the alternative basic load combinations are used. ■ 4. Load Case 2 — Wind / Seismic Reversal Bottom row tension · top edge bearing Enter ASD service-level loads for the reversed case. The reversed reaction puts the BOTTOM anchor row in couple tension and bearing at the TOP plate edge. Do NOT pre-reduce wind by 0.6 here — use the ⅓ increase checkbox instead. V2 — Beam Reaction Shear (kips) Total reversed reaction delivered to the plate (all 4 anchors). P2 — Axial Pull-out (kips) Uniform tension/pull-out shared by all 4 anchors (P/4 each). Apply ⅓ allowable stress increase (IBC 1605.2) to Case 2 — k = 3/4 on the demand side of every check. Typically applicable for wind/seismic-governed combinations. ▶ Run All Checks ✓ EMBED PLATE DESIGN ADEQUATE Max D/C = 0.937 — governs: Part D Interaction — Case 1 — Gravity · Top Row f′m = 2.5 ksi · ϕ = 0.75″ · Lb = 6.00″ · Sx = 5.0″ · d = 9.00″ · Ba = 4.621 k · Bv = 3.119 k (Crushing (Bv2)) · ⅓ increase: C1 & C2 ▮ PART A — Geometry & Embedment · TMS 402-22 §6.3.6 / Eq 6-7 ✓ PASS — Geometry & Minimum Embedment · Max D/C = 0.500 0.75″ Anchor Diameter ϕ 0.3345 in² Net Area Aₔ (Eq 6-7) 6.00″ Embedment Lᵦ provided 3.00″ Lᵦ,min = max(4ϕ,2″) 9.00″ Row Spacing d = Hₚ-eₜ-eᵇ 73.94 in² A₝t (per anchor) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Net Threaded Area Ab nt = 10.0 tpi (UNC) TMS 402-22 Eq 6-7 ϕnet = 0.6526″ Ab = 0.3345 in² — Info ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §6.3.8 / Eq 6-7 — Net Threaded Effective Area Ab nt = 10.0 tpi — UNC coarse standard (ASME B1.1) for ϕ = 0.75″; set automatically from the diameter, not a user input. Ab = (π/4)(ϕ − 0.9743/n t )² = (π/4)(0.750 − 0.9743/10.0)² = (π/4)(0.6526)² = 0.3345 in² (net — more conservative than gross πϕ²/4 = 0.442 in²) Min Embedment Lb ϕ = 0.75″ TMS 402-22 §6.3.6 Lb = 6.00″ Lb,min = 3.00″ 0.500 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §6.3.6 — Minimum Anchor Bolt Embedment Lb,min = MAX[4ϕ, 2″] = MAX[4×0.75, 2] = 3.00″ Lb,provided = 6.00″ ≥ L b,min ✓ D/C = 3.00 6.00 = 0.500 Projected Areas Apt, Apv Lb=6.00″, Sx=5.0″, d=9.00″ TMS 402-22 §6.3.2/§6.3.3 Apt = 73.94 in² Apv = 142.88 in² — Info ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §6.3.2 / Eq 6-5 — Projected Tension Area Apt (per anchor, wall-face plane) Aseg(R, c) = R²·acos(c/R) − c·√ R² − c² — area of a circle beyond a chord at distance c from center; half-lens deduction per neighbor per §6.3.2. πLb² = π×6.00² = 113.10 in² (full cone, no free edge) Deductions (one neighbor of each kind for a 2×2 group): Aseg(Lb, c = Sx/2 = 2.50″) = L b ²·acos( 2.50 6.00 ) − 2.50·√ Lb² − 2.50² = 27.44 in² — in-row neighbor Aseg(Lb, c = d/2 = 4.50″) = L b ²·acos( 4.50 6.00 ) − 4.50·√ Lb² − 4.50² = 8.16 in² — cross-row neighbor g = √ Sx² + d² = √ 5.00² + 9.00² = 10.296″ (diagonal spacing) Aseg(Lb, c = g/2 = 5.15″) = L b ²·acos( 5.15 6.00 ) − 5.15·√ Lb² − 5.15² = 3.56 in² — diagonal neighbor Aseg(Lb, c = lbe,shear = 12.00″) = L b ²·acos( 12.00 6.00 ) − 12.00·√ Lb² − 12.00² = 0.00 in² — c ≥ L b , so = 0 (no deduction) — free-edge deduction Apt = 113.10 − 27.44 − 8.16 − 3.56 − 0.00 = 73.94 in² Apv = πl be ²/2 − 0.5·A seg (l be , S x /2) — in-row neighbor only 0.5·Aseg(lbe, Sx/2) = 0.5×[ l be ²·acos( 2.50 12.00 ) − 2.50·√ lbe² − 2.50² ] = 83.32 in² Apv = π×12.00²/2 − 83.32 = 226.19 − 83.32 = 142.88 in² Cross-row shear-cone interaction is not deducted: the row nearest the free edge governs, and the far row’s larger cone envelops it — evaluated per the governing row. With a remote edge (≥ ~2·l be from anchors) breakout will not govern; verify when a close edge or opening exists. ▮ PART B — Anchor Tension · TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 ✓ PASS — Anchor Tension (governing Ba = 4.621 k) · Max D/C = 0.243 1.500 k C1 Top-row T (per bolt, raw) 1.125 k C1 Demand k·T (k=3/4) 0.600 k C2 Bot-row T (per bolt, raw) 4.621 k Bₐ Masonry 1.25A₝t√f'm 9.699 k Bₐ Steel 0.5AₔFᵤ 4.621 k Bₐ Governing min(mas,steel) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Tension — Top Row (Case 1 — Gravity) Tcouple=3.000 k; Apt=73.94 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 k·T = 1.125 k Ba = 4.621 k 0.243 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 — Anchor Tension (Case 1 — Gravity · Top Row) M = V·e = 15.00×2.00 = 30.00 k-in ; T couple = M/y = 30.00/10.00 = 3.000 k T = P/4 + T couple /2 = 0.00/4 + 3.000/2 = 1.500 k/bolt (raw) k·T = 0.75×1.500 = 1.125 k (demand) k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) Ba,mas = 1.25 A pt √ f′m = 1.25×73.94×√ 2500.0 /1000 = 4.621 k Ba,steel = 0.5 A b F u = 0.5×0.3345×58.0 = 9.699 k ⇒ B a = min = 4.621 k D/C = 1.125 4.621 = 0.243 Tension — Bottom Row (Case 1 — Gravity) P/4 only (P=0) TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 k·To = 0.000 k Ba = 4.621 k 0.000 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 — Anchor Tension (Case 1 — Gravity · Bottom Row, shear-side row) To = max(P/4, 0) = max(0.00/4, 0) = 0.000 k/bolt (no couple tension in this row) k·To = 0.75×0.000 = 0.000 k Demand = 0 ⇒ D/C = 0.000 (row carries shear only; shown for completeness) Tension — Bottom Row (Case 2 — Reversal) Tcouple=1.200 k; Apt=73.94 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 k·T = 0.450 k Ba = 4.621 k 0.097 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 — Anchor Tension (Case 2 — Reversal · Bottom Row) M = V·e = 6.00×2.00 = 12.00 k-in ; T couple = M/y = 12.00/10.00 = 1.200 k T = P/4 + T couple /2 = 0.00/4 + 1.200/2 = 0.600 k/bolt (raw) k·T = 0.75×0.600 = 0.450 k (demand) k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) Ba,mas = 1.25 A pt √ f′m = 1.25×73.94×√ 2500.0 /1000 = 4.621 k Ba,steel = 0.5 A b F u = 0.5×0.3345×58.0 = 9.699 k ⇒ B a = min = 4.621 k D/C = 0.450 4.621 = 0.097 Tension — Top Row (Case 2 — Reversal) P/4 only (P=0) TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 k·To = 0.000 k Ba = 4.621 k 0.000 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 — Anchor Tension (Case 2 — Reversal · Top Row, shear-side row) To = max(P/4, 0) = max(0.00/4, 0) = 0.000 k/bolt (no couple tension in this row) k·To = 0.75×0.000 = 0.000 k Demand = 0 ⇒ D/C = 0.000 (row carries shear only; shown for completeness) ▮ PART C — Anchor Shear · TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 ✓ PASS — Anchor Shear (governing Bv = 3.119 k · Crushing (Bv2)) · Max D/C = 0.902 3.750 k C1 v = V₁/4 (per bolt) 1.500 k C2 v = V₂/4 (per bolt) 8.930 k Bᵥ1 Mas. Breakout 3.119 k Bᵥ2 Crushing 9.243 k Bᵥ3 Pryout 4.850 k Bᵥ4 Steel 3.119 k Bᵥ Governing Crushing (Bv2) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Shear — all 4 anchors (Case 1 — Gravity) governs: Crushing (Bv2) TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 k·v = 2.813 k Bv = 3.119 k 0.902 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 — Anchor Shear (Case 1 — Gravity, all 4 anchors) v = V/4 = 15.00/4 = 3.750 k/bolt (raw) ; k·v = 0.75×3.750 = 2.813 k k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) Bv1 = 1.25 A pv √ f′m = 1.25×142.88×√ 2500.0 /1000 = 8.930 k (masonry breakout) [Eq 8-4] Bv2 = 580(A b f′ m ) 0.25 = 580×(0.3345×2500.0) 0.25 /1000 = 3.119 k (crushing) [Eq 8-5] (580 is the code’s empirical coefficient, lb/psi units — Ab in in², f′m in psi, result converted to kips) Bv3 = 2.5 A pt √ f′m = 2.5×73.94×√ 2500.0 /1000 = 9.243 k (pryout) [Eq 8-6] Bv4 = 0.25 A b F u = 0.25×0.3345×58.0 = 4.850 k (steel) Bv = min = 3.119 k — governs: Crushing (B v2 ) D/C = 2.813 3.119 = 0.902 Shear — all 4 anchors (Case 2 — Reversal) governs: Crushing (Bv2) TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 k·v = 1.125 k Bv = 3.119 k 0.361 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 — Anchor Shear (Case 2 — Reversal, all 4 anchors) v = V/4 = 6.00/4 = 1.500 k/bolt (raw) ; k·v = 0.75×1.500 = 1.125 k k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) Bv1 = 1.25 A pv √ f′m = 1.25×142.88×√ 2500.0 /1000 = 8.930 k (masonry breakout) [Eq 8-4] Bv2 = 580(A b f′ m ) 0.25 = 580×(0.3345×2500.0) 0.25 /1000 = 3.119 k (crushing) [Eq 8-5] (580 is the code’s empirical coefficient, lb/psi units — Ab in in², f′m in psi, result converted to kips) Bv3 = 2.5 A pt √ f′m = 2.5×73.94×√ 2500.0 /1000 = 9.243 k (pryout) [Eq 8-6] Bv4 = 0.25 A b F u = 0.25×0.3345×58.0 = 4.850 k (steel) Bv = min = 3.119 k — governs: Crushing (B v2 ) D/C = 1.125 3.119 = 0.361 ▮ PART D — Combined Interaction · TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.3 / Eq 8-8 ✓ PASS — Combined Interaction (Eq 8-8 — tension row each case; opposite row when P > 0) · Max D/C = 0.937 0.937 C1 Top-row interaction 0.203 C2 Bot-row interaction 4.621 k Bₐ Governing 3.119 k Bᵥ Governing 1.00 Interaction Limit Eq 8-8 Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Interaction — Top Row (Case 1 — Gravity) Ba=4.621 k, Bv=3.119 k TMS 402-22 Eq 8-8 (kT/Ba)5/3+(kv/Bv)5/3 = 0.937 ≤ 1.0 0.937 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.3 / Eq 8-8 — Combined Tension + Shear (Case 1 — Gravity · Top Row) (kT/Ba)5/3 + (kv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0 = ( 1.125 4.621 ) 5/3 + ( 2.813 3.119 ) 5/3 = 0.095 + 0.842 = 0.937 ≤ 1.0 ✓ k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) D/C = 0.937 1.00 = 0.937 Interaction — Bottom Row (Case 2 — Reversal) Ba=4.621 k, Bv=3.119 k TMS 402-22 Eq 8-8 (kT/Ba)5/3+(kv/Bv)5/3 = 0.203 ≤ 1.0 0.203 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.3 / Eq 8-8 — Combined Tension + Shear (Case 2 — Reversal · Bottom Row) (kT/Ba)5/3 + (kv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0 = ( 0.450 4.621 ) 5/3 + ( 1.125 3.119 ) 5/3 = 0.021 + 0.183 = 0.203 ≤ 1.0 ✓ k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) D/C = 0.203 1.00 = 0.203 ▮ PART E — Masonry Bearing · TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 / §4.4.4 ✓ PASS — Masonry Bearing (Fbr = 0.33f′m = 0.825 ksi) · Max D/C = 0.341 0.281 ksi C1 fᵇᵣ (bottom edge) 0.112 ksi C2 fᵇᵣ (top edge) 0.825 ksi Fᵇᵣ = 0.33f'm 3.000 k C1 C = Tᴄᴏᵤₚₗₑ 1.200 k C2 C = Tᴄᴏᵤₚₗₑ Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Bearing at bottom edge (Case 1 — Gravity) C=3.000 k over Wp×ac TMS 402-22 §8.1.6/§4.4.4 fbr = 0.281 ksi Fbr = 0.825 ksi 0.341 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 / §4.4.4 — Masonry Bearing (Case 1 — Gravity, bearing at bottom edge) C = T couple = 3.000 k ; k·C = 0.75×3.000 = 2.250 k k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) fbr = k·C/(W p ·a c ) = 2.250/(8.00×1.00) = 0.281 ksi (bearing at bottom edge) Fbr = 0.33 f′ m = 0.33×2.50 = 0.825 ksi D/C = 0.281 0.825 = 0.341 Bearing at top edge (Case 2 — Reversal) C=1.200 k over Wp×ac TMS 402-22 §8.1.6/§4.4.4 fbr = 0.112 ksi Fbr = 0.825 ksi 0.136 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 / §4.4.4 — Masonry Bearing (Case 2 — Reversal, bearing at top edge) C = T couple = 1.200 k ; k·C = 0.75×1.200 = 0.900 k k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) fbr = k·C/(W p ·a c ) = 0.900/(8.00×1.00) = 0.112 ksi (bearing at top edge) Fbr = 0.33 f′ m = 0.33×2.50 = 0.825 ksi D/C = 0.112 0.825 = 0.136 ▮ PART F — Plate Bending & Shear · AISC 360-22 §F11 / §G ✓ PASS — Plate Bending & Shear (Fy,plate = 36.0 ksi) · Max D/C = 0.703 15.19 ksi C1 fᵇ bending 6.08 ksi C2 fᵇ bending 21.60 ksi Fᵇ = 0.60Fᵧ 2.813 ksi C1 fᵥ shear 1.125 ksi C2 fᵥ shear 14.40 ksi Fᵥ = 0.40Fᵧ Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Plate Bending — bolt-tension model fb (Case 1 — Gravity) Trow=3.000 k, a=4.500″, K=0.5 AISC 360-22 §F11 fb = 15.19 ksi Fb = 21.60 ksi 0.703 PASS ▶ Calc AISC 360-22 §F11 — Plate Bending, Bolt-Tension Fixed-Fixed Model (Case 1 — Gravity) Trow = 2×T bolt = 2×1.500 = 3.000 k (governing-row raw per-bolt tension × 2 bolts) a = H p /2 − e t = 12.00/2 − 1.50 = 4.500″ (governing tension row to plate centroid) K = 0.50 — fixed-fixed: the anchor rows at each end create fixed conditions; the plate cantilevers to the center from each way. Mpl = K·T row ·a = 0.50×3.000×4.500 = 6.750 k-in ; k·M pl = 0.75×6.750 = 5.063 k-in k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) S = W p t²/6 = 8.00×0.500²/6 = 0.3333 in³ fb = k·M pl /S = 5.063/0.3333 = 15.19 ksi Fb = 0.60 F y,plate = 0.60×36.0 = 21.60 ksi D/C = 15.188 21.600 = 0.703 Plate Shear fv (Case 1 — Gravity) max(V,Trow)=15.000 k over Wp×t AISC 360-22 §G2 fv = 2.813 ksi Fv = 14.40 ksi 0.195 PASS ▶ Calc AISC 360-22 §G — Plate Shear (Case 1 — Gravity) V = 15.00 k ; T row = 3.000 k ⇒ governing = max(V, T row ) = 15.000 k (V governs) k·max(V,Trow) = 0.75×15.000 = 11.250 k k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) fv = k·max(V,T row )/(W p ·t) = 11.250/(8.00×0.500) = 2.813 ksi Fv = 0.40 F y,plate = 0.40×36.0 = 14.40 ksi D/C = 2.813 14.400 = 0.195 F11 Applicability & Model Basis full Wp assumed effective — see detail AISC 360-22 §F11.1 Weak-axis bending Mn = Mp (no LTB); Fb = 0.60Fy conservative — Info ▶ Calc AISC 360-22 §F11 — Applicability (weak-axis rectangular plate model) The plate is modeled as a rectangular bar bent about its minor (weak) axis — the plate thickness t lies in the plane of bending. The §F11.2 lateral-torsional buckling provisions (Eqs. F11-2/F11-3, parameter L b d/t²) apply to rectangular bars bent about their major axis only; for minor-axis bending M n = M p with no LTB reduction (§F11.1). The applied allowable F b = 0.60F y on the elastic modulus S = W p t²/6 is more conservative than §F11.1 (M n = M p ≤ 1.6M y , i.e. φ/Ω-adjusted ≈ 0.9F y ·S). Model idealization: the full plate width W p is assumed effective in bending; local plate bending at the beam weld footprint and load spreading are not evaluated here — engineering judgment required for narrow weld groups on wide plates. Plate Bending — bolt-tension model fb (Case 2 — Reversal) Trow=1.200 k, a=4.500″, K=0.5 AISC 360-22 §F11 fb = 6.08 ksi Fb = 21.60 ksi 0.281 PASS ▶ Calc AISC 360-22 §F11 — Plate Bending, Bolt-Tension Fixed-Fixed Model (Case 2 — Reversal) Trow = 2×T bolt = 2×0.600 = 1.200 k (governing-row raw per-bolt tension × 2 bolts) a = H p /2 − e b = 12.00/2 − 1.50 = 4.500″ (governing tension row to plate centroid) K = 0.50 — fixed-fixed: the anchor rows at each end create fixed conditions; the plate cantilevers to the center from each way. Mpl = K·T row ·a = 0.50×1.200×4.500 = 2.700 k-in ; k·M pl = 0.75×2.700 = 2.025 k-in k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) S = W p t²/6 = 8.00×0.500²/6 = 0.3333 in³ fb = k·M pl /S = 2.025/0.3333 = 6.08 ksi Fb = 0.60 F y,plate = 0.60×36.0 = 21.60 ksi D/C = 6.075 21.600 = 0.281 Plate Shear fv (Case 2 — Reversal) max(V,Trow)=6.000 k over Wp×t AISC 360-22 §G2 fv = 1.125 ksi Fv = 14.40 ksi 0.078 PASS ▶ Calc AISC 360-22 §G — Plate Shear (Case 2 — Reversal) V = 6.00 k ; T row = 1.200 k ⇒ governing = max(V, T row ) = 6.000 k (V governs) k·max(V,Trow) = 0.75×6.000 = 4.500 k k = 0.75 (⅓ increase per IBC 1605.2 applied on demand) fv = k·max(V,T row )/(W p ·t) = 4.500/(8.00×0.500) = 1.125 ksi Fv = 0.40 F y,plate = 0.40×36.0 = 14.40 ksi D/C = 1.125 14.400 = 0.078 Engineering Notes: Design basis: ASD per TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 (anchorage), §8.1.6/§4.4.4 (bearing), and AISC 360-22 §F11/§G (plate). 4-anchor 2×2 configuration only; anchors embedded horizontally into the wall face. Statics: V splits equally to all 4 anchors (rigid group, v = V/4). The V·e moment is resisted by a couple — one tension anchor row + direct plate bearing at the opposite edge; anchors carry no compression. Uniform axial P is shared P/4 to every anchor and added to the tension-row couple demand. Two cases: Case 1 (Gravity/Down) — top row tension, bearing at bottom edge, y = d + eb − ac/2. Case 2 (Wind/Seismic Reversal) — bottom row tension, bearing at top edge, y = d + et − ac/2. Governing row flips between cases. Net threaded area: Ab = (π/4)(ϕ − 0.9743/nt)² per Eq 6-7 (§6.3.8) — deliberately more conservative than gross πϕ²/4. Projected areas (wall-face plane): Apt = πLb² less circular-segment deductions for all three neighbor overlaps of the 2×2 group — in-row circSeg(Lb, Sx/2), cross-row circSeg(Lb, d/2), and diagonal circSeg(Lb, g/2) with g = √(Sx²+d²) — each ARE deducted per §6.3.2 (one neighbor of each kind per anchor, half-lens each), plus circSeg(Lb, lbe,shear) for the free edge. No auto-deduction for top-of-wall, control joints, or openings. Tension (Ba): min of masonry breakout 1.25Apt√f′m and steel 0.5AbFu. Fu capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi). Shear (Bv): min of masonry breakout Bv1 = 1.25Apv√f′m (Eq 8-4, always evaluated using the entered lbe,shear), crushing 580(Abf′m)0.25 (Eq 8-5), pryout 2.5Apt√f′m (Eq 8-6), and steel 0.25AbFu. Apv = πlbe²/2 − 0.5·circSeg(lbe, Sx/2) — in-row neighbor only. Cross-row shear-cone interaction is not deducted: the row nearest the free edge governs, and the far row’s larger cone envelops it — evaluated per the governing row. With a remote edge (≥ ~2·lbe from anchors) breakout will not govern; verify when a close edge or opening exists. Interaction (Eq 8-8): (kT/Ba)5/3 + (kv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0, evaluated on the tension row of each case, plus the opposite row when P > 0 puts it in net tension. When P = 0 the opposite row sees equal shear and zero tension, so its interaction reduces to the Part C shear check and can never govern. Bearing: fbr = k·C/(Wp·ac) ≤ Fbr = 0.33f′m at the compression edge; ac is an assumed bearing-block depth. Plate bending — bolt-tension fixed-fixed model (per EOR direction): the plate is driven by the governing anchor row tension, not the beam eccentricity. Trow = 2×(per-bolt raw tension); the lever arm a = Hp/2 − et (Case 1) or Hp/2 − eb (Case 2) is the distance from the tension row to the plate centroid. With K = 0.5 (fixed-fixed — the two anchor rows fix the plate, which cantilevers to the center from each way), Mpl = K·Trow·a and fb = k·Mpl/(Wpt²/6) ≤ 0.60Fy. Plate shear fv = k·max(V, Trow)/(Wpt) ≤ 0.40Fy. §F11 LTB applies to major-axis bar bending only — not applicable to this weak-axis model; Fb = 0.60Fy on S is conservative vs Mn = Mp. Full plate width Wp assumed effective; local bending at the beam weld footprint is not evaluated — use judgment for narrow weld groups on wide plates. Anchor-couple statics unchanged: e remains the anchor-couple eccentricity only — it drives M = V·e and the couple tension Tcouple = M/y, but no longer serves as a plate cantilever length. Weld design is out of scope. ⅓ stress increase (IBC 1605.2): applied per case as k = 3/4 on the DEMAND side of every check (tension, shear, interaction, bearing, plate) — capacities unchanged. Applies only when the alternative basic load combinations of IBC 1605.2 are used (including for the steel plate allowables); engineer must verify eligibility.

audit.dimensions: PASS: Sx, d, plate, Lb and standoff labels match fixture/math
